title: pterocereus gaumeri subsp. foetidus
intro: Pterocereus gaumeri subsp. foetidus, also known as the foetid pitaya, is a succulent plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family native to the Yucatan Peninsula in Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant compounds that have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and cold, as well as to treat wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Pterocereus gaumeri subsp. foetidus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
